Online Data Table V: Intergenerational Mobility Estimates by Commuting Zone

Notes: This table presents estimates of intergenerational mobility by CZ. Estimates are reported only for CZs with more than 250 children in the relevant samples. Relative mobility (RM) is the slope from OLS regression of child rank on parent rank within each CZ. Relative mobility can be multiplied by the difference in parent ranks (on a 0-100 scale) to obtain the expected difference in child's rank (on a 0-100 scale).   Absolute upward mobility (AM) is the expected rank of children whose parents are at the 25th percentile of the national income distribution. Expected ranks at other percentiles P between 0 and 100 can be computed as AM+(P-25)*RM. Variable descriptions are as follows:
